title: The asymmetry of the methodology landscape
desc: Most design and product methodologies cluster in the upper layers of the system. Once placed on a single map, the structural asymmetry becomes impossible to ignore.

title: Level 7 is not uniform — three phenomena the field treats as one
desc: Architectural contradictions, real forks, and positional constraints look identical from the outside. Confusing them produces three structurally different kinds of failure.

title: Trialogue as phenomenon
desc: A configuration of human + AI + AI resolves a different contradiction than dialogue. The gap between two AIs makes the frame visible — and opens structural access to the positional level.

title: Write-access as architectural constraint in multi-agent research workflows
desc: A live case: the absence of shared writable storage as a productive architectural constraint. Analysed through CDPA. The constraint forced infrastructure into existence — and makes the next step visible.
